event: The Captives after the Fall of Antioch
description: A monochrome engraving depicting the taking of captives after the fall of Antioch during the Crusades. At center and right, armored or richly robed commanders and soldiers stand on an elevated platform or steps. Below them, a group of women, some with children, kneel or stand in attitudes of supplication and despair. The women wear flowing robes and head coverings. A text caption at bottom reads about the capture of Antioch and the selling of women and children as slaves. The composition contrasts the power of the armed men above with the vulnerability of the captives below.
